name: Wild Rice and Barley Pilaf
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 12-inch frying pan or 2- to 3-quart pan, combine onion, mushrooms, garlic, and 1/2 cup water.
2. Cook, uncovered, on high heat until the liquid evaporates and a brown film forms in the pan, about 15 minutes. Stir often.
3. Add 2 or 3 tablespoons water and stir to free the brown film; cook until the film forms again.
4. Repeat this step 4 or 5 times until onions are richly browned, about 15 minutes.
5. Rinse and drain rice. Mix with broth in the pan. Bring to a boil on high heat; cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
6. Rinse and drain barley. Add to rice; simmer until grains are tender but just slightly chewy, about 20 minutes longer.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Makes 5 or 6 servings.
